2606.21020.title: CheXpercept: A Benchmark for Evaluating Expert-Level Lesion Perception in Chest X-rays
2606.21020.paper_summary: The evaluation of vision-language models (VLMs) for chest X-ray (CXR) analysis has largely been limited to disease-presence classification without visual grounding. Such evaluations fail to verify the expert-level lesion perception necessary to ensure the clinical reliability of VLMs. To address these limitations, we introduce CheXpercept, a sequential, multi-level perception benchmark that mirrors a radiologist's cognitive workflow across coarse-level detection, fine-level contour evaluation and revision, and semantic-level attribute extraction. To ensure high clinical fidelity at scale, we construct the dataset using a semi-automated generation pipeline paired with a review by six medical experts. CheXpercept contains 10,400 QA items derived from 2,100 CXRs, covering seven clinically critical pulmonary and cardiac lesions. To demonstrate the current landscape of VLM perception, we benchmark 14 general and medical VLMs on CheXpercept. The models achieve adequate performance only at the coarse level, with accuracy degrading precipitously on deeper visual tasks. Notably, medical VLMs show almost no perceptual advantage over their general-domain counterparts, highlighting a systemic flaw in current domain adaptation. The code and dataset will be publicly available.
2606.21020.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型 (VLMs) 在胸部 X 光片 (CXR) 分析的評估大多僅限於疾病存在的分類，且未進行視覺基礎的驗證。這樣的評估未能驗證專家級的病變感知，這對於確保 VLMs 的臨床可靠性是必要的。為了解決這些限制，我們推出了 CheXpercept，一個序列式的多層次感知基準，模擬放射科醫生在粗略檢測、細緻輪廓評估和修訂以及語義層次屬性提取過程中的認知工作流程。為了確保在大規模下的高臨床真實性，我們使用半自動生成管道構建數據集，並由六位醫學專家進行審核。CheXpercept 包含 10,400 個來自 2,100 份 CXR 的 QA 項目，涵蓋七種臨床關鍵的肺部和心臟病變。為了展示當前 VLM 感知的現狀，我們在 CheXpercept 上對 14 個通用和醫學 VLM 進行基準測試。這些模型僅在粗略層次上達到適當的性能，並且在更深層次的視覺任務中準確率急劇下降。值得注意的是，醫學 VLM 幾乎沒有相對於其通用領域對應物的感知優勢，突顯了當前領域適應中的系統性缺陷。代碼和數據集將公開提供。
2606.21020.author: Geon Choi et.al.
2606.21020.authors: Geon Choi, Hangyul Yoon, Nalee Kim, Jeong Yun Jang, Hyunju Shin, Hyunki Park, Sang Hoon Seo, Edward Choi
